payload.name: RAG Console Frontend
payload.description: Build a production-ready enterprise web frontend for the Production RAG backend, exposing Copilot Q&A, AI Observability traces/logs, and Document ingestion/management in a unified single-page application that matches the PRODUCT.md design principles.

goal.answer: Build a production-ready enterprise web frontend for the Production RAG backend that exposes all three product tabs — Copilot Q&A, AI Observability traces/logs, and Document ingestion/management — in a unified single-page application matching the PRODUCT.md design principles.

payload.plain: Build a production-ready enterprise web frontend for the Production RAG backend that exposes all three product tabs — Copilot Q&A, AI Observability traces/logs, and Document ingestion/management — in a unified single-page application matching the PRODUCT.md design principles.

criteria.answer:
Three-tab shell (Copilot, Observability, Documents) routes and renders without runtime errors
Copilot supports streaming (/ask/stream) and non-streaming (/ask) queries and displays answer, route, evidence status, citations, SQL, and result rows
Observability supports searching traces, viewing a trace waterfall/spans, and viewing correlated logs
Documents supports file upload, status tracking, version update, delete, and a document list
Backend exposes a GET /documents list endpoint so the Documents tab can enumerate documents (only backend change required)
Frontend meets WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility targets (keyboard navigation, visible focus, labelled inputs, aria-live)
Frontend builds successfully and passes type checking, lint, and smoke tests
Backend CORS already permits frontend origin (http://localhost:3000)

plain: Three-tab shell (Copilot, Observability, Documents) routes and renders without runtime errors
Copilot supports streaming (/ask/stream) and non-streaming (/ask) queries and displays answer, route, evidence status, citations, SQL, and result rows
Observability supports searching traces, viewing a trace waterfall/spans, and viewing correlated logs
Documents supports file upload, status tracking, version update, delete, and a document list
Backend exposes a GET /documents list endpoint so the Documents tab can enumerate documents (only backend change required)
Frontend meets WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility targets (keyboard navigation, visible focus, labelled inputs, aria-live)
Frontend builds successfully and passes type checking, lint, and smoke tests
Backend CORS already permits frontend origin (http://localhost:3000)

constraints.answer: Frontend stack: Vite + React 18+ + TypeScript + TanStack Router + TanStack Query + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui, Single-user internal tool; do not build auth, billing, or notification surfaces, Backend changes are limited to adding a GET /documents list endpoint; no other backend refactoring, Design must follow PRODUCT.md principles: honest states, evidence-first, teal signal, dense readable data, All UI text/data must come from real backend responses; no fabricated metrics or progress

plain: Frontend stack: Vite + React 18+ + TypeScript + TanStack Router + TanStack Query + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
Single-user internal tool; do not build auth, billing, or notification surfaces
Backend changes are limited to adding a GET /documents list endpoint; no other backend refactoring
Design must follow PRODUCT.md principles: honest states, evidence-first, teal signal, dense readable data
All UI text/data must come from real backend responses; no fabricated metrics or progress

phases.answer: Design & spec, Scaffold & core, Copilot tab, Observability tab, Documents tab, Integration & polish, Final verification

phaseSnapshots.name: Design & spec
phaseSnapshots.goal: Confirm architecture, write approved design spec

steps.description: Write design spec with stack rationale, component architecture, data flow, accessibility plan, and API mappings

phaseSnapshots.name: Scaffold & core
phaseSnapshots.goal: Create the frontend project shell, API client, theme tokens, and layout

steps.description: Scaffold Vite + React + TypeScript project and configure Tailwind, shadcn/ui, TanStack Router, TanStack Query, and lint/test tooling
Implement type-safe API client for backend endpoints and SSE streaming parser
Implement design system, theme tokens, and accessible layout shell with three-tab navigation
Build chat input, message list, route badge, evidence status, and citation cards
Integrate /ask/stream SSE endpoint with meta/status/delta/final events and non-streaming fallback

phaseSnapshots.name: Observability tab
phaseSnapshots.goal: Implement trace search and trace/log inspection

steps.description: Build trace search filters and result table with pagination
Build trace waterfall/spans view and correlated logs panel

phaseSnapshots.name: Documents tab
phaseSnapshots.goal: Implement document upload, list, and lifecycle actions

steps.description: Add GET /documents list endpoint to backend and implement document list with status, update, delete
Build upload dropzone with progress, status display, and async polling

phaseSnapshots.name: Integration & polish
phaseSnapshots.goal: Wire tabs together, add error handling, loading/empty/failure states, and accessibility

steps.description: Connect tabs to router, add global error boundaries, and polish responsive behavior
Run accessibility checks and verify keyboard navigation and aria-live announcements

phaseSnapshots.name: Final verification
phaseSnapshots.goal: Confirm production build and document usage

steps.description: Run production build and end-to-end smoke test against running backend
Write README with run instructions and architecture notes

assumptions.answer: Backend API runs at http://localhost:8000; Frontend dev server runs at http://localhost:3000 (CORS already allowed in api.py); Node.js and pnpm (or npm) are available in the environment; The user will review and approve the design spec before implementation begins; Document records are persisted in the backend and can be listed by adding a GET /documents endpoint

payload.summary: Phase 1 complete: the RAG Console Frontend design spec has been written and verified at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-29-rag-console-frontend-design.md (217 lines). It covers architecture, stack rationale, project structure, data flow, API mappings, component design per tab, accessibility, error/loading/empty states, testing strategy, required backend change, and open questions.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.
